book_claims.claim: Shiitake mushrooms are used across multiple Longevity Diet dishes and provide 41 IU of vitamin D per cup cooked (10.25% DV). Mushrooms synthesize vitamin D upon UV light exposure, making them a significant dietary vitamin D source for those limiting animal products.
book_claims.mechanism: Mushrooms convert ergosterol to vitamin D2 (ergocalciferol) upon UV/sunlight exposure; dietary vitamin D2 raises serum 25(OH)D, supporting immune regulation, bone metabolism, and cardiovascular health.

supplementary_research.finding: Daily consumption of 5–10 g dried shiitake mushrooms for 4 weeks improved markers of immunity including T-cell proliferation, NK cell activity, secretory IgA levels, and reduced C-reactive protein — suggesting immunomodulatory longevity benefits beyond vitamin D content.

supplementary_research.source: Wasser, Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2002) — PMID 12012168
supplementary_research.finding: Shiitake's beta-glucan (lentinan) activates macrophages and NK cells, enhances tumor surveillance, and modulates Th1/Th2 balance — mechanisms relevant to immune aging and cancer prevention in longevity contexts.

supplementary_research.source: Kabir et al., Journal of Nutritional Science and Vitaminology (1987) — PMID 3437695
supplementary_research.finding: Shiitake consumption significantly reduced total cholesterol and LDL in animal models via eritadenine, a unique compound that shifts cholesterol metabolism — supporting cardiovascular aspects of the longevity diet.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: ~18 IU (cooked, standard); up to 46,000 IU if UV-treated
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: UV-exposed shiitake dramatically increases vitamin D2; raising serum 25(OH)D is effective though D2 may be slightly less potent than D3 at equivalent doses. Store gill-side up in sunlight to maximize D content.
Lentinan is a 1,3/1,6-beta-glucan with high immunostimulatory potency; partially resistant to digestion allowing interaction with intestinal immune cells (Peyer's patches).
Unique shiitake compound that modifies phospholipid metabolism and reduces LDL cholesterol; bioavailable orally with activity demonstrated at dietary doses in human studies.
Organic selenomethionine form; contributes to antioxidant enzyme cofactor pool.

synergies.description: Shiitake beta-glucans and garlic allicin both enhance NK cell activity and macrophage function via distinct immune receptors — complementary immunostimulatory longevity effects.

Shiitake's vitamin D promotes calcium absorption and immune regulation while spinach provides iron and folate; together they address multiple micronutrient gaps common in plant-forward longevity diets.
